headline: Amgen (AMGN) Recalls Put Its Valuation Story Back In Focus
summary: Amgen (AMGN) has moved into focus after initiating voluntary nationwide recalls of certain Corlanor and Sensipar tablet lots due to foreign substances and CGMP deviations, prompting investors to reassess drug quality and compliance risks. See our latest analysis for Amgen. Against this recall backdrop, Amgen’s recent momentum has stayed intact, with a 30 day share price return of 5.30% and a year to date share price return of 12.35%.
